prompt: Translate this English proverb into two other languages in a way that preserves the spirit, not the words: 'The best time to plant a tree was twenty years ago. The second best time is now.' Add a one-sentence note on each.
response:
Spanish  
“La mejor época para sembrar un sueño fue ayer; la segunda mejor es este mismo instante.”  
Note: Keeps the forward-looking energy while swapping “tree” for “sueño” to resonate in cultures where hope matters more than timber.

Swahili  
“Mti ulipopandwa jana ni mbegu tuliyokosa; leo tuna nafasi nyingine ya kupanda.”  
Note: Reframes the proverb around collective opportunity, stressing that yesterday’s loss becomes today’s shared second chance.
